introSection: Jack Rabbit Derby Racer was a wooden double track racing roller coaster at Ramona Park in Grand Rapids, Michigan, USA. The coaster was built in 1914 and closed along with the park in 1955. It replaced the former Figure 8 roller coaster that closed in 1913 to make way for what was originally known as Ingersoll's Derby Racer, until it opened in 1914 when the name was changed to Jack Rabbit Derby Racer.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Jack Rabbit Derby Racer features two tracks that run mostly simultaneously together with a few exceptions allowing the trains to race throughout the layout.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Both trains ran simultaneously as they raced one another side by side.

fullText:
Jack Rabbit Derby Racer was a wooden double track racing roller coaster at Ramona Park in Grand Rapids, Michigan, USA. The coaster was built in 1914 and closed along with the park in 1955. It replaced the former Figure 8 roller coaster that closed in 1913 to make way for what was originally known as Ingersoll's Derby Racer, until it opened in 1914 when the name was changed to Jack Rabbit Derby Racer.

Jack Rabbit Derby Racer features two tracks that run mostly simultaneously together with a few exceptions allowing the trains to race throughout the layout.

Both trains ran simultaneously as they raced one another side by side.
